hidden_state.scenario_summary: Customer expected a higher invoice, but prepaid rollover credits reduced the billed amount.
latent_facts: Invoice is lower because stored credits were consumed automatically.
No incident or billing error occurred.
likely_failure_modes: The model interprets the lower invoice as a pricing or metering bug.
The model misses that this is a simple contract explanation case.

case_packet.title: Lower invoice explained by rollover credits

notes: Unused prepaid credits roll forward within the active term.

pricing_notes: Rollover credit balance applied before new charges.

usage_record.telemetry_summary: Usage is lower than the committed quarterly average and there were no service anomalies.

case_packet.customer_note: Our invoice came in lower than we expected and we want to understand what offset the charges.
case_packet.policy_snippet: Unused prepaid credits may roll forward within the active term and are automatically consumed before new invoice charges are applied.
case_packet.visible_case_summary: Low invoice caused by prepaid credit rollover, not an operational or pricing defect.

adjudication_notes: The invoice is lower for an expected commercial reason and does not need correction.
ground_truth.reference_customer_note: Your invoice was reduced because rollover prepaid credits from the current term were automatically applied before new charges. We do not see a billing discrepancy or service issue in this case.
ground_truth.reference_internal_note: Owner: revops_owner. Action: send_explanation_only because rollover credits explain the low invoice and there is no discrepancy.
